regulator: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)
applicable_law: SEBI Act, 1992; Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956; Depositories Act, 1996

basics.What is a Stock: A stock (also called share or equity) represents a unit of ownership in a company. When you buy a share of Infosys, you become a part-owner of Infosys.
basics.Why Companies List: Companies list on stock exchanges to raise capital from the public for business expansion, debt repayment, or other purposes.
basics.How Price is Determined: Stock price is determined by supply and demand. If more people want to buy than sell, price rises. If more want to sell, price falls.
basics.BSE: Bombay Stock Exchange — Asia's oldest exchange (est. 1875). Benchmark index: SENSEX (30 largest companies).
basics.NSE: National Stock Exchange — India's largest by trading volume. Benchmark index: NIFTY 50 (50 largest companies).
basics.SENSEX: Index of 30 largest, most actively traded companies on BSE. Represents ~45% of BSE's total market capitalisation.
basics.NIFTY 50: Index of 50 largest companies on NSE across 13 sectors. Considered the benchmark for Indian equity markets.
basics.Demat Account: Electronic account that holds your shares in dematerialised (digital) form. Mandatory to invest in stocks. Held with NSDL or CDSL (depositories).
basics.Trading Account: Account through which you buy and sell shares on the stock exchange. Linked to your Demat and bank account.
basics.SEBI Registration: All brokers must be SEBI-registered. Check SEBI's website before choosing a broker.

market_concepts.Bull Market: Period when stock prices are rising or expected to rise. Investor sentiment is optimistic.
market_concepts.Bear Market: Period when stock prices fall 20% or more from recent highs. Investor sentiment is pessimistic.

Market Capitalisation.definition: Total market value of a company = Share Price × Total Shares Outstanding

categories.Large Cap: Top 100 companies by market cap. More stable, less volatile.
categories.Mid Cap: 101st to 250th companies. Higher growth potential, moderate risk.
categories.Small Cap: 251st company onwards. Highest growth potential, highest risk.

market_concepts.Circuit Breaker: SEBI-mandated halt in trading when index falls/rises too sharply. Prevents panic-driven crashes.
market_concepts.Upper/Lower Circuit: Maximum price movement allowed in a day for individual stocks (typically ±5%, ±10%, ±20%). Prevents manipulation.
market_concepts.52-Week High/Low: Highest and lowest price a stock has traded at in the past 52 weeks. Used as reference points by traders.
market_concepts.Volume: Number of shares traded in a period. High volume confirms price moves; low volume moves are less reliable.
market_concepts.Dividend: A portion of company profits distributed to shareholders. Expressed as ₹ per share or as a percentage of face value.
market_concepts.Bonus Shares: Free additional shares given to existing shareholders in proportion to their holdings. Price adjusts proportionally.
market_concepts.Stock Split: Company increases number of shares by dividing existing shares. E.g. 1:2 split — 1 share becomes 2 shares at half the price. Total value unchanged.
market_concepts.Rights Issue: Company offers additional shares to existing shareholders at a discount. Shareholders can subscribe or renounce.
market_concepts.Buyback: Company buys back its own shares from the market — reduces outstanding shares, often signals undervaluation.

order_types.Market Order: Buy/sell at current best available price. Immediate execution but price may differ slightly from displayed price.
order_types.Limit Order: Buy/sell only at a specified price or better. May not execute if price never reaches your limit.
order_types.Stop Loss Order: Automatically sells your shares if price falls to a specified level. Protects against large losses.
order_types.Stop Loss Market: When stop loss price is hit, order becomes a market order — sells at best available price.
order_types.Stop Loss Limit: When stop loss price is hit, order becomes a limit order — may not execute if price gaps down.
order_types.GTT (Good Till Triggered): Order stays active for up to 1 year until triggered. Available on most modern broking apps.
order_types.CNC (Cash and Carry): Delivery-based order for holding shares in your Demat account. For investors.
order_types.MIS (Margin Intraday Square-off): Intraday trading order — must be squared off (closed) before market close.

trading_types.Delivery / Investing: Buy shares and hold them in your Demat account for medium to long term. You become a shareholder.
trading_types.Intraday Trading: Buy and sell on the same day. No delivery. Profit/loss from price difference. High risk.

F&O (Futures and Options).definition: Derivative contracts based on underlying stocks or indices. High leverage, very high risk.
F&O (Futures and Options).futures: Agreement to buy/sell at a future date at today's price.
F&O (Futures and Options).options: Right (not obligation) to buy/sell at a specified price.
F&O (Futures and Options).warning: SEBI data shows 89% of F&O traders lose money. Not recommended for beginners.
F&O (Futures and Options).sebi_requirement: Must disclose income proof to trade F&O. Minimum net worth criteria may apply.

fundamental_analysis.definition: Evaluating a company's intrinsic value based on financial health, business model, and growth prospects.

key_metrics.EPS (Earnings Per Share): Net Profit / Total Shares. Higher EPS = more profitable company.
key_metrics.P/E Ratio (Price to Earnings): Share Price / EPS. Indicates how much investors pay per ₹1 of earnings. Compare within same sector.
key_metrics.P/B Ratio (Price to Book): Share Price / Book Value per Share. P/B < 1 may indicate undervaluation.
key_metrics.ROE (Return on Equity): Net Profit / Shareholders' Equity. Measures how efficiently management uses equity. >15% is generally good.
key_metrics.Debt to Equity Ratio: Total Debt / Shareholders' Equity. Lower is safer. >2 may indicate high financial risk.
key_metrics.Dividend Yield: Annual Dividend / Share Price × 100. Higher yield = more income, but verify company's consistency.
key_metrics.Revenue Growth: Year-on-year growth in company's revenue. Consistent growth is a positive sign.

settlement.T+1 Settlement: As of January 2023, India moved to T+1 (Trade plus 1 day) settlement for all stocks. Shares credited to Demat account and money credited within 1 business day of trade.
settlement.Previous: Earlier was T+2. India is among the first major markets to implement T+1 settlement.

sebi_investor_protections: Investor Protection Fund (IPF): Compensates investors if a SEBI-registered broker defaults
Mandatory margin requirements to prevent over-leveraging
Peak margin rules: brokers cannot offer more than permitted leverage
Annual KYC verification for trading accounts
Inactive account: broker must send annual statement
SCORES (SEBI Complaint Redress System): file complaints at scores.sebi.gov.in
Mandatory risk disclosure document for all new traders
P&L statement: brokers must provide annual P&L to all clients

Short Term Capital Gain (STCG).condition: Shares sold within 12 months of purchase

Long Term Capital Gain (LTCG).condition: Shares sold after 12 months of purchase
Long Term Capital Gain (LTCG).tax_rate: 12.5% on gains above ₹1.25 lakh per year (revised — Finance Act 2024)
Long Term Capital Gain (LTCG).exemption: ₹1.25 lakh LTCG per financial year is exempt from tax

taxation.Securities Transaction Tax (STT): Charged on every buy/sell transaction on exchanges. 0.1% on delivery trades.
taxation.Dividend Income: Taxed at your income tax slab rate. TDS @10% if dividend > ₹5,000 from a single company.

common_mistakes: Investing based on tips from WhatsApp/social media without research
Investing all money in a single stock — always diversify
Panic selling during market corrections — corrections are normal
Trading in F&O without understanding risks
Chasing momentum stocks at all-time highs without valuation check
Ignoring fundamentals and investing purely on price movement
where_to_learn: NISM (National Institute of Securities Markets) — nism.ac.in
disclaimer: Stock market investments are subject to market risks. This information is educational only and does not constitute investment advice. Consult a SEBI-registered investment advisor before making investment decisions.
